pages.title: Useful Commands
pages.text: Players and admins have tools available:$(br2)$(li)$(bold)/reload$() Refresh datapacks$(li)$(bold)/give @s patchouli:guide_book{patchouli:book:"botzmobleveling:mob_leveling_player_guide"}$() Replace this book$(li)$(bold)/give @s patchouli:guide_book{patchouli:book:"botzmobleveling:mob_leveling_developer_guide"}$() Get the dev guide$(br2)Operators can use Minecraft's standard entity commands to inspect mob data.

pages.title: Debug Mode
pages.text: When enabled in config, debug mode logs detailed information:$(br2)$(li)Which rule won for each spawned mob$(li)Level calculation steps$(li)Attribute modifications applied$(br2)This is invaluable for datapack authors testing their configurations.$(br2)$(o)Only enable temporarily-it's verbose and fills logs quickly.

pages.title: Checking Mob Data
pages.text: To inspect a specific mob's level:$(br2)$(li)Look for the level indicator above its head$(li)Check the mob's data through commands$(li)Note the spawn location and check what rules would apply there$(br2)This helps verify that your datapacks are working as intended.

pages.title: Getting Help
pages.text: If things aren't working as expected:$(br2)$(li)Check the developer guide's troubleshooting section$(li)Verify your JSON syntax (commas, braces, quotes)$(li)Confirm file paths match expected structure$(li)Enable debug mode for detailed logs$(li)Test with /reload after every change$(br2)Most issues are simple syntax errors or incorrect file locations.
